title: fMRI Anticipation vs. Experience Study: Dissociating Somatic Marker Retrieval from Prediction Error
description: This fMRI study uses a risky-choice task with variable foreperiod to test whether somatic marker retrieval M̂(c,a) modulates effective interoceptive precision Πⁱ_eff during anticipation, rather than directly encoding the raw prediction error εⁱ during outcome. APGI predicts that vmPFC–posterior insula connectivity emerges during the anticipation window (before stimulus onset), not the outcome window, and that this coupling is sensitive to option valence rather than sensory contrast. Four sub-predictions are tested: that M̂ modulates Πⁱ_eff rather than εⁱ directly (P5a), that vmPFC–posterior insula coupling peaks during anticipation (P5b), that the vmPFC effect is context-specific (valence-sensitive, not contrast-sensitive) (P5c), and that suppressing anticipation via a no-foreperiod control collapses vmPFC–insula coupling (P5d). Identifiability of β and Πⁱ is fully resolved via orthogonal fMRI + SCR channels confirmed by block-diagonal Fisher Information Matrix in pre-session protocol.
prediction_cluster_label: Dissociating Somatic Marker Retrieval from Prediction Error

sub_predictions.claim: M̂ modulates Πⁱ_eff, not εⁱ directly
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: vmPFC–posterior insula functional connectivity during anticipation period; vmPFC BOLD does not correlate with primary interoceptive prediction error εⁱ at outcome
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: vmPFC BOLD correlates with primary εⁱ (outcome-locked SCR) rather than with anticipatory connectivity

sub_predictions.claim: vmPFC–posterior insula coupling appears during anticipation window, not outcome window
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: PPI (psychophysiological interaction) analysis shows vmPFC–posterior insula connectivity peak before stimulus onset (anticipation > outcome, p < 0.05 FWE-corrected)
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: No anticipatory vmPFC–posterior insula coupling; vmPFC–insula connectivity limited to outcome period

sub_predictions.claim: Somatic marker effect is context-specific: sensitive to option valence, not sensory contrast
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: vmPFC activation parametrically modulated by option expected value (EV) across option valence, not by Gabor contrast or sensory luminance
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: vmPFC activation correlates with sensory contrast or physical stimulus attributes regardless of option valence

sub_predictions.claim: Removing anticipation (no-foreperiod control) collapses vmPFC–insula coupling
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: Significantly reduced vmPFC–posterior insula coupling in 0 ms foreperiod condition vs. 2000–4000 ms foreperiod condition (paired t-test on PPI coefficient, p < 0.05)
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: vmPFC–insula coupling persists at equivalent magnitude under no-anticipation control

validation_notes: Identifiability fully resolved. β and Πⁱ orthogonality confirmed via block-diagonal Fisher Information Matrix from orthogonal fMRI + SCR channels in pre-session protocol. No submission-blocking issues for this protocol.

inclusion_criteria: Willingness to participate in risky-choice decision task
exclusion_criteria: Diabetes or autonomic neuropathy (would affect SCR)

apparatus.fMRI_sequence: Multi-band EPI (MB factor 4), TR = 1000 ms, TE = 30 ms, voxel size 2 mm isotropic, 72 axial slices
apparatus.structural_MRI: T1-weighted MPRAGE, 1 mm isotropic, for anatomical localisation
apparatus.SCR: MRI-compatible Biopac MP160 with 8 mm Ag/AgCl electrodes, thenar/hypothenar placement, 2000 Hz sampling
apparatus.cardiac: MRI-compatible pulse oximeter (Siemens PMU) for cardiac noise correction
apparatus.display: Fiber-optic goggle system (Resonance Technology) at 60 Hz, 800 × 600 resolution

target.type: Risky choice: two-option gamble display (expected value 0.2–0.8, variance 0.1–0.6)
target.presentation: Left-right option pair; valence indicated by colour (gain = green, loss = red)

trial_structure.response: Left or right button press within 3000 ms decision window; outcome revealed after 1500 ms delay

blocks.condition_counterbalancing: Foreperiod length (0 vs. 2000–4000 ms) blocked within runs; option valence pseudorandomised across trials

fmri_analysis_pipeline.motion_correction: ICA-AROMA denoising; scrubbing at FD > 0.5 mm
regressors_of_interest: Anticipation period (onset = option display, duration = foreperiod length)
Outcome period (onset = outcome reveal, duration = 1 s)
Parametric: option expected value (EV) × anticipation period
fmri_analysis_pipeline.nuisance_regressors: 6 motion parameters, CSF signal, WM signal, cardiac and respiratory noise (RETROICOR)
rois: vmPFC (medial prefrontal cortex): anatomical mask from Neurovault vmPFC atlas
Anterior insula (aIC): anatomical mask from AAL2 atlas
Posterior insula (pIC): anatomical mask from AAL2 atlas
fmri_analysis_pipeline.connectivity_analysis: Psychophysiological interaction (PPI): seed = vmPFC, target = posterior insula; psychological regressor = anticipation vs. outcome contrast

identifiability_protocol.description: Pre-session protocol uses alternating fMRI and SCR-only blocks to estimate β (somatic modulation gain) and Πⁱ (interoceptive precision) from orthogonal measurement channels. FIM off-diagonal block confirmed < 0.1 (near-zero covariance between β and Πⁱ estimates), confirming identifiability.

dependent_variables: vmPFC BOLD: anticipation period parametric modulation by EV
vmPFC–posterior insula PPI coefficient: anticipation > outcome contrast
vmPFC–anterior insula PPI coefficient: anticipation > outcome contrast
vmPFC activation: valence-specificity (gain vs. loss option foreperiod)
PPI coefficient: long foreperiod vs. no foreperiod (0 ms) condition
Estimated β and Πⁱ from pre-session FIM protocol
primary_hypothesis: vmPFC–posterior insula functional connectivity peaks during the anticipation foreperiod rather than the outcome period, and this anticipatory coupling is parametrically modulated by option expected value (valence) rather than sensory contrast, consistent with somatic marker retrieval M̂ operating on Πⁱ_eff prior to decision execution.
primary_analysis: ROI-based PPI analysis: vmPFC–pIC coupling coefficient compared between anticipation and outcome regressors (paired t-test across participants, p < 0.05 FWE-corrected). Secondary: whole-brain EV parametric modulation during anticipation. Foreperiod manipulation (P5d): mixed-effects with foreperiod length as within-participant factor.

power_analysis.based_on: Naqvi et al. (2007) vmPFC-insula in somatic marker tasks; typical PPI effect sizes in anticipation paradigms

caveats: SCR is a noisy proxy for εⁱ; individual differences in SCR reactivity may reduce power for P5a. Include SCR-reactor subgroup analysis (top 50% SCR amplitude).
vmPFC coordinates vary across atlas conventions; report results in both AAL2 and Schaefer parcellation for reproducibility.
Beta-blockers must be excluded to preserve SCR signal; this may introduce selection bias toward lower-anxiety participants.
